business_ideas.idea: Reputation management for individuals online (like Reputation.com, Yext)
Do Not Pay-style service for reputation/privacy issues (class action suits, deletion)
Social media monitoring for company employees (flag controversial posts pre-emptively)
TikTok clip creation/aggregation service (monetize podcast through clips)

predictions.text: Crypto/Web3 talent influx creates self-fulfilling prophecy—projects will succeed due to engineering talent.
Solana and Layer 2 solutions will continue explosive growth as viable Ethereum alternatives.

disagreements: Sam concerned about reputation management being 'soul sucking'; Shaan sees it as technically fascinating but ethically grey. Both agree crypto narrative is powerful regardless of fundamentals.
dynamics: Sam anchors with specific business problems (right-to-be-forgotten, reputation destruction); Shaan goes deep on crypto valuations and talent migration. Sam shares personal jealousy about friends' crypto wealth; Shaan reframes it as mentorship/luck observation. Both discuss how privilege of knowing successful people creates normalcy bias around wealth building.
frameworks: Talent arbitrage — Best people move to opportunities; their concentration signals where future value accrues.
Self-fulfilling prophecy — Massive VC/talent allocation to crypto makes success inevitable regardless of current use cases.
Wealth amnesia — Watching friends build brick-by-brick creates respect; sudden wealth from crypto creates jealousy (lottery vs. hard work).
learnings: Google's autocomplete dominates search reputation more than first-page results; manipulation requires beating both.
Reputation.com charges $100K-$120K for enterprise clients; most individuals cannot afford reputation defense.
Right-to-be-forgotten (EU law) doesn't exist in US; digital mistakes are permanent career damage.
Crypto is attracting top talent from BigTech (Amazon VP, Google CPO, Coinbase CFO, Twitter/Square); self-reinforcing cycle.
Wealth can be created insanely fast in crypto: $250K→$1B (Solana), $500K→$15M (DeFi), half-million→$15M (individual trader).
Normal people with no trading background can turn life savings into life-changing money through crypto (not lottery-like, but rare).
Silicon Valley's magic is not just talent but density—coffee shops full of constant pitching/meeting culture normalizes entrepreneurship.

banger_quotes.quote: You can't have this much talent spend all day thinking about this without something coming from it.
There's a self-fulfilling prophecy in crypto—talent + capital guarantees eventual product-market fit.
Your success reminds them of their failure; they hate their lack, not you.
TikTok creators understand the vibe; our employees don't know why Sam's talking about his monthly eggs.

numbers_dropped: MFM TikTok clips: 15M impressions in 10-15 days
22-year-old protocol creator: Market cap $900M → $200M, personal extraction $10M, donated $7M to charity
Coinbase IPO beneficiary: CPO stake worth $600M
Moiz Ali (Native Deodorant): started to $100M exit in 2 years
notable: Episode documents MFM's viral TikTok moment (15M impressions) and pivots to discussing crypto wealth creation as a parallel phenomenon. Shaan proposes talent migration to crypto as proof of inevitable market creation; Sam expresses unguarded jealousy about friends' unexpected wealth gains. Includes detailed breakdown of reputation.com's business model (Google manipulation, autocomplete clearing).
